citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
On 12/2/2025, an animal caretaker failed to euthanize a juvenile mouse following the method described in the protocol
approved by the IACUC. Failure to follow the approved method of euthanasia may cause pain or distress before death.
The Animal Welfare Regulations require that proposals to conduct activities involving animals contain a description of any
euthanasia method to be used. Any proposed changes must be submitted to and approved by the IACUC before
implementation. Staff received refresher training on protocol compliance and humane euthanasia standards to prevent
recurrence. Corrected prior to inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives.
End of report.
n

narrative:
2.31(e)(5)  Critical
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
On 12/2/2025, an animal caretaker failed to euthanize a juvenile mouse following the method described in the protocol 
approved by the IACUC. Failure to follow the approved method of euthanasia may cause pain or distress before death. 
The Animal Welfare Regulations require that proposals to conduct activities involving animals contain a description of any 
euthanasia method to be used. Any proposed changes must be submitted to and approved by the IACUC before 
implementation. Staff received refresher training on protocol compliance and humane euthanasia standards to prevent 
recurrence. Corrected prior to inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives. 
 
End of report.
